title: glandularia pulchella
intro: Glandularia pulchella, also known as the pink verbena, is a flowering plant native to South America, characterized by its small pink flowers and a fragrance reminiscent of rose geranium. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Glandularia pulchella include its potential to ease anxiety and stress, as well as its antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of Glandularia pulchella include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to promote relaxation and well-being.
